_description: Strong technical builder, advanced AI/backend stack, no competition wins, no paying customers, no formal lab. Expected: Internship Tier 3, College Score 3, Entrepreneurship Tier 4, Research Tier 3.

conversation.content: Hi Alex! I've had a chance to look over your resume — impressive technical stack for a junior. I'd love to hear more about what you've actually been building. What's the most complex project you've worked on, and what problem were you trying to solve?

Yeah, so for the past three months I've been building this AI tutoring app. It uses a multi-agent pipeline — one agent breaks down the student's question, another retrieves relevant content from a vector store, and a third one synthesizes an explanation tailored to the student's level. The backend is FastAPI with async endpoints, and I'm using LangChain for the agent orchestration. I built the whole thing myself.

That's a genuinely sophisticated architecture — most people don't think through the retrieval and synthesis separation at your stage. Who's actually using it right now? And what made you choose AI tutoring specifically?

Honestly just me and a couple friends testing it. I haven't launched publicly yet — I'm still polishing the UI. I chose tutoring because I struggled a lot with AP Physics and felt like existing tools like Khan Academy were too generic. I wanted something that actually adapts to how you think, not just what you got wrong.

That's a clear personal pain point, which is a good foundation. Let me ask about the bigger picture — where do you see yourself in five years? Are you more drawn to building products like this, going deep into research on how these models work, or something else entirely?

I think I want to go to a strong CS program — ideally MIT or Carnegie Mellon — and then work at a company like Anthropic or OpenAI. I'm really interested in how LLMs reason, like why chain-of-thought prompting works. I've read a few papers on it — the original chain-of-thought paper by Wei et al. and some work on scratchpad reasoning — but I haven't done any formal research. I'd love to do research eventually but I don't even know where to start.
That intellectual curiosity comes through clearly, and knowing specific papers puts you ahead of most. One last thing — tell me about what you're most excited to work on next after the tutoring app.

I want to add a feature that tracks how a student's understanding evolves over multiple sessions — like a longitudinal model of their knowledge gaps. And longer term I want to open-source the agent framework I built so other people can build on it.

top_repos.name: ai-tutoring-app
top_repos.description: Multi-agent AI tutoring pipeline with RAG backend
